Tomato Early Blight.organic_solution: Remove infected lower leaves, improve airflow, and spray diluted neem oil every 5 to 7 days.
Tomato Early Blight.chemical_solution: Apply a mancozeb-based fungicide according to the label directions.

Powdery Mildew.organic_solution: Use a baking soda spray or neem oil and avoid overhead watering late in the day.
Powdery Mildew.chemical_solution: Use a sulfur or potassium bicarbonate fungicide as recommended for the crop.

Leaf Spot.organic_solution: Prune affected leaves, sanitize tools, and use compost tea or copper soap carefully.
Leaf Spot.chemical_solution: Spray a copper oxychloride or chlorothalonil-based fungicide as per guidance.

Bacterial Wilt.organic_solution: Remove severely affected plants, rotate crops, and improve soil drainage to reduce spread.
Bacterial Wilt.chemical_solution: Use a recommended bactericide where applicable and disinfect tools after field work.

Healthy.organic_solution: The plant looks healthy. Maintain mulching, balanced compost use, and regular scouting.
Healthy.chemical_solution: No chemical treatment is needed right now. Continue preventive farm hygiene.

Unknown.organic_solution: Capture a clearer leaf image in good lighting and monitor the plant for 2 to 3 days.
Unknown.chemical_solution: Avoid spraying chemicals until symptoms are better identified.
